name: Set Up a Utility Payment Plan to Avoid Disconnection
description: Call a utility (electric / gas / water) when a shutoff notice has arrived (or is imminent) and negotiate a Deferred Payment Arrangement (DPA), hardship credit, or LIHEAP referral so service stays on while the balance is paid down over time.
disclaimer: If service has already been disconnected, ask immediately about reconnection fees and same-day vs. next-day reconnection windows. If a household member is medically vulnerable or the weather is in a state-defined cold/heat-shutoff-moratorium window, say so first — most states legally prohibit disconnection in those cases.

context.when_to_use: User has received a disconnect / shutoff notice, OR sees a past-due balance large enough that they cannot pay in full this cycle, OR has already been disconnected. Time-sensitive — call before the disconnect date on the notice, not after.
preconditions: User has the shutoff notice with disconnect date and amount due.
User knows what they can realistically pay TODAY and what they can pay per month going forward.
User knows whether the household qualifies for state/federal energy assistance (LIHEAP, ratepayer assistance) — even rough income bracket helps.
User has documented any medical or weather-vulnerability factors.

principles: Call BEFORE the disconnect date. Pre-disconnect, you negotiate; post-disconnect, you pay reconnect fees and a deposit on top of arrears.

There are three layers of help, request in order: (1) Deferred Payment Arrangement, (2) hardship credit / one-time forgiveness, (3) LIHEAP / state energy-assistance referral. Don't lead with assistance — DPAs require no income verification.

Mention vulnerability factors EARLY (medical, infants under 1, seniors over 62, active-duty military, recent job loss). Many utilities have program codes that only activate when the rep ticks the right box.
If routed to collections instead of customer-care / billing assistance, recognise it within 60 seconds — collections agents are scripted on payment-in-full, not on plans.
Be specific with numbers. 'I can pay $X today and $Y per month for 6 months' is a proposal; 'I'm struggling, what can you do?' is a wish.

phrases.opener: Hi, I received a shutoff notice on account [number] for $[amount] dated [disconnect date]. I want to keep service on — I can pay $[amount] today, and I'd like to set up a deferred payment arrangement for the balance. Can we walk through options?
phrases.queue_check: Quick check — am I in customer care / payment assistance, or in collections? I'm here to set up a payment plan, and I want to make sure I'm with the team that can authorise one.
phrases.transfer_request: Could you do a warm transfer to the payment-arrangement team and stay on the line until they pick up? I don't want to lose my hold position if they can't take me right now.
phrases.vulnerability_flag: Before we go further — there's [an infant under 1 / a senior over 62 / someone on powered medical equipment / recent job loss] in the household. Does that change the options available on this account?
phrases.weather_moratorium_check: Is this a [cold weather / heat] moratorium period in [state]? I'd like to understand whether disconnect can proceed before [moratorium end date] regardless of the plan.
phrases.propose_dpa: Here's what I can do: $[down payment] today, then $[monthly] on top of the regular bill for [N] months. That clears the arrears in [N] months. Does that work, or what does your standard DPA look like?
phrases.ask_hardship_credit: Does the company have a one-time hardship credit or arrears-forgiveness program I could apply for? I'd rather match my plan with that than borrow to catch up.
phrases.ask_liheap: Can you refer me to LIHEAP / [state-specific assistance program] and put a 14-day hold on disconnect while my application is processed? Most programs guarantee that hold once you have an application reference number.
phrases.lock_in_writing: Can you email or text me the DPA terms — down payment, monthly add-on, end date, and confirmation that disconnect is removed — before we hang up? I want it in writing.
phrases.graceful_close: Thanks. So down payment of $[X] today, then $[Y]/month for [N] months, disconnect lifted, confirmation number [number], and I'll watch for the email.

tactics.name: Lead with a specific proposal
tactics.script: Use `propose_dpa`. Naming the down payment + the monthly add-on first anchors the negotiation. Most utility DPA defaults are 25% down + balance over 6-12 months — proposing inside that envelope gets fast approval.

tactics.name: Recognise wrong-queue fast
tactics.when: Rep starts pushing for payment-in-full or threatens 'we cannot stop the disconnect'.
tactics.script: Use `queue_check` and `transfer_request`. Collections is incentivised on collection rate; payment-assistance is incentivised on retention.

tactics.name: Surface vulnerability factors early
tactics.when: Within the first 2 minutes if any apply.
tactics.script: Use `vulnerability_flag`. This is not leverage — it changes which program codes the rep can apply.

tactics.name: Check the weather moratorium
tactics.script: Use `weather_moratorium_check`. If active, disconnect is often legally barred — that buys time even if the DPA negotiation stalls.

tactics.name: Layer hardship + assistance referrals
tactics.when: DPA alone leaves a monthly burden the user cannot sustain.
tactics.script: Use `ask_hardship_credit` AND `ask_liheap`. The DPA covers arrears; assistance can knock down the principal so the monthly is sustainable.

tactics.name: Get it in writing before hanging up
tactics.script: Use `lock_in_writing`. Verbal DPAs evaporate at shift change. Confirmation email + case number is the proof you need if collections action restarts.

boundaries: Do NOT agree to a monthly add-on the user cannot actually pay. Default-breaking a DPA usually triggers immediate disconnect with no second chance.
Do NOT pay anything before the rep has confirmed the DPA is set up in the system — paying down the arrears voluntarily can be applied to current charges, leaving the past-due intact.
Do NOT misrepresent income for hardship credit. Most programs verify and a fraudulent claim disqualifies the account from future assistance.
Do NOT cancel autopay during a DPA — autopay default often disables the DPA automatically.
success_signals: DPA terms confirmed: down payment, monthly add-on, end date, removal of disconnect.
Confirmation number issued and written confirmation incoming.
Vulnerability flag accepted; medical-baseline or shutoff-protection added to account.
LIHEAP / assistance referral with a hold-on-disconnect window.
failure_signals: Rep insists on payment in full and refuses to transfer to assistance.
DPA monthly add-on offered is larger than the user can sustain — accepting would set up a default.
Disconnect proceeds despite confirmed moratorium (escalate to PUC same day).
No written confirmation arrives within 48 hours.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm all numbers, the rep's name, confirmation number, and the email/text channel for written terms. Calendar each monthly DPA payment.
exit_strategy.on_failure: File a complaint with the state PUC same day — include the rep name, time, and substance of refusal. Simultaneously, contact local community-action agency or 211 for emergency utility assistance funding (one-time bill payment) and any utility ombudsman.
follow_ups: Confirm written DPA terms received within 48 hours.
Pay the down payment immediately, not at the end of the day.
If LIHEAP referred, complete the application within the hold window (typically 14 days).
Calendar each monthly add-on — DPA default usually triggers immediate shutoff without further notice.

required_user_info: Amount user can pay monthly beyond regular bill
Any vulnerability factors (medical, infant, senior, military, recent job loss)
Whether income may qualify for LIHEAP / state assistance
Best email or phone for written confirmation
